summary: A clean, behavior-preserving enabling refactor. printSysstat/printPgstat were reduced to thin *gocui.View wrappers delegating to new renderSysstat/renderPgstat(w io.Writer, ...), faithfully mirroring the existing printDbstat -> renderDbstat precedent. Every fmt.Fprintf/Fprintln moved verbatim with only the v -> w receiver substituted; format strings, argument order, ANSI codes, and newlines are unchanged, so compact output stays byte-identical. New golden tests cover both panels and pass.

suggestions.suggestion: renderSysstat keeps the original `var err error` + repeated `_, err = ...` style, while renderPgstat uses `_, err := ...` on its first line. Both are correct; this is just a stylistic asymmetry inherited from the pre-refactor code. Leaving it as-is is the right call for a behavior-preserving refactor — no change recommended, noted only for completeness.
suggestions.benefit: None material; flagged purely for transparency.

notes.behaviorPreservation: Verified byte-for-byte. Each of the four Fprintf lines in renderSysstat and the Fprintln + three Fprintf lines in renderPgstat are identical to the originals except the io.Writer receiver. No verbose rows added, matching the task scope (verbose composers are Task 8).
notes.precedentConformance: Naming (renderSysstat/renderPgstat) matches the tech-spec Task 3 contract and the renderDbstat pattern. Wrappers pass v directly since *gocui.View implements io.Writer, exactly as printDbstat does. Doc comments on both wrappers correctly explain the delegation and cite the renderDbstat precedent.
notes.crossFileConsistency: Call sites in printStat (lines 133, 143) are untouched and still invoke the unchanged wrapper signatures. Test structs use real fields: stat.Stat embeds System (LoadAvg/CPUStat/Meminfo) and Pgstat (Activity); construction via stat.Stat{System: ...} and stat.Stat{Pgstat: stat.Pgstat{Activity: ...}} compiles and matches existing usage in the test file.
notes.testing: Test_renderSysstat_compact and Test_renderPgstat_compact assert the 4 compact lines against a bytes.Buffer. Line 1 (timestamp) is correctly matched by regexp since it is non-deterministic; pgstat line 1 is tied to formatInfoString for consistency with Test_formatInfoString. Lines 2-4 asserted byte-for-byte including \033[37;1m ANSI codes. go test ./top/... is green; go vet clean.
notes.verification: go test ./top/... passed; go vet ./top/... clean. New tests pass on the refactored code, confirming the golden-output invariant.
